skill.name: firecrawl-crawl
skill.description: Bulk extract content from an entire website or site section. Use this skill when the user wants to crawl a site, extract all pages from a docs section, bulk-scrape multiple pages following links, or says "crawl", "get all the pages", "extract everything under /docs", "bulk extract", or needs content from many pages on the same site. Handles depth limits, path filtering, and concurrent extraction.
skill.summary: Bulk extract content from entire websites or site sections with depth limits and path filtering.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Security review of firecrawl-crawl skill completed. Static analysis flagged multiple patterns (external_commands, filesystem path traversal, weak_crypto, network_reconnaissance) that were all evaluated as false positives. The flagged content consists of documentation examples in markdown code blocks and relative markdown links to other skill files, not actual security risks. The skill is a legitimate web scraping tool that uses the firecrawl CLI to crawl and extract content from websites.

medium_findings.title: External Command Documentation Examples
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged bash command examples in SKILL.md as 'external_commands'. These are documentation examples in markdown code blocks showing how to use the firecrawl CLI, not actual code execution. No command injection risk exists in this documentation file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation file contains bash command examples in code blocks - standard skill documentation format with no actual code execution

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Path Traversal in Markdown Links
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged relative path references in markdown links as 'path traversal'. These are valid relative links to other skill documentation files (e.g., '../firecrawl-scrape/SKILL.md'), which is standard documentation cross-referencing, not filesystem access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown relative links to documentation files are standard documentation practice, not filesystem operations

content.user_title: Crawl entire websites for bulk content extraction
content.value_statement: Manually extracting content from hundreds of pages is time-consuming. This skill automates bulk web crawling with depth limits and path filtering to extract exactly the content you need.
actual_capabilities: Crawl entire websites or specific site sections following links
Set maximum depth limits to control how far links are followed
Filter URLs by include and exclude path patterns
Extract content from hundreds of pages in a single operation
Control crawl speed with delay and concurrency settings
Save results to JSON files for further processing
limitations: Credit-based pricing - large crawls consume API credits per page
Cannot crawl password-protected or login-required content
JavaScript-heavy sites may require firecrawl-scrape for complete extraction

use_cases.title: Documentation site migration
use_cases.description: Extract all content from a documentation site before migration or redesign. Use --include-paths /docs to scope the crawl to documentation sections only.
use_cases.target_user: Developers migrating documentation to new platforms

use_cases.title: Competitive analysis research
use_cases.description: Bulk extract product pages, pricing information, and feature lists from competitor websites for market research and analysis.

use_cases.title: Knowledge base building
use_cases.description: Build a local knowledge base by crawling and extracting content from multiple related websites, wikis, or resource collections.

prompt_templates.title: Basic site crawl
prompt_templates.prompt: Use firecrawl-crawl to extract all pages from [URL]. Save the results to crawl-results.json
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need all content from a simple website

prompt_templates.title: Scoped section crawl
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl [URL] but only extract pages under the /docs/ path. Limit to 50 pages maximum.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need content from a specific section only

prompt_templates.title: Deep crawl with depth control
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl [URL] with a maximum depth of 3 links. Use --include-paths /products /blog to filter to relevant sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need structured content with depth limits

prompt_templates.title: Large-scale extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Crawl [URL] extracting up to 200 pages from the /resources/ section. Use --delay 500ms to be respectful. Save output to resources-crawl.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need bulk extraction with rate limiting

output_examples.input: Crawl all pages under /docs/ from example.com
output_examples.output:
Successfully crawled 47 pages from example.com/docs/

Sample pages extracted:
- /docs/getting-started (2,340 words)
- /docs/api-reference (4,120 words)
- /docs/tutorials/basics (1,890 words)

Results saved to: .firecrawl/crawl.json

Credits used: 47

output_examples.input: Extract product pages from an e-commerce site
output_examples.output:
Crawl complete: 156 pages extracted from example-store.com

Categories found:
- /products/electronics (42 pages)
- /products/clothing (38 pages)
- /products/home (76 pages)

Output written to: products-crawl.json

best_practices: Always use --include-paths to scope crawls to relevant sections - avoid crawling entire sites when you only need specific content
Use --wait to ensure crawl completes before processing results - without it, the command returns a job ID for async polling
Check credit usage with 'firecrawl credit-usage' before starting large crawls to avoid unexpected costs
anti_patterns: Do not crawl entire large websites without path filtering - this wastes credits and generates irrelevant content
Do not skip --wait for time-sensitive tasks - async job IDs require manual polling and add complexity
Do not set extremely high concurrency without considering rate limits - respect target website servers

faq.question: What is the difference between crawl and scrape?
faq.answer: Scrape extracts a single page. Crawl discovers and extracts all pages by following links, with depth and path controls.

faq.question: How does pricing work for crawling?
faq.answer: Crawling uses credits per page crawled. Check your credit balance with 'firecrawl credit-usage' before large operations.

faq.question: Can I crawl password-protected content?
faq.answer: Standard crawling does not support authentication. For protected content, use the API directly with proper authentication.

faq.question: How do I resume an interrupted crawl?
faq.answer: Crawls are job-based. Use 'firecrawl crawl <job-id>' to check status of a running or interrupted crawl.

faq.question: What happens if a site blocks the crawl?
faq.answer: Use --delay to add time between requests. If blocked, the crawl will stop and report partial results.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: Results are saved as JSON by default. Use --pretty for formatted output. The -o flag specifies the output file path.
